title: Logo style refresh, hero text color update, and design polish

requirements.text: Redesign the AP monogram logo in the Hero section with an improved background — use a rich, earthy or premium color (e.g., deep green, warm amber, or coffee brown) as the logo background shape, with high-contrast lettering. Ensure the logo mark feels polished and brand-appropriate for an agricultural/dairy business.

quotes: Adjust the logo colors or style

acceptanceCriteria: The AP logo in the Hero has a visually distinct, well-styled background (circle, shield, or badge shape).
Logo background color is drawn from the existing earthy/agri palette (earth greens, coffee browns, or warm ambers).
The 'AP' initials are clearly legible with strong contrast against the logo background.
The logo does not look plain or flat — it has a premium, finished appearance.

requirements.text: Update the hero section's company name, tagline, and any supporting text to use a clearly visible, high-contrast font color that reads well over the hero background image. Prefer warm white, cream, or a light earthy tone rather than the default theme foreground.

quotes: Change the font color of the hero text

acceptanceCriteria: The company name and tagline text in Hero.tsx are legible and contrast well with the background image.
Text color is explicitly set (not relying on the default theme variable that may be invisible over the image).
No hero text appears washed out or invisible on any common screen.

requirements.text: Apply overall design polish across the site: ensure color consistency between the logo, hero, navbar, and section headings using the established earthy/agri palette; tighten spacing and typography hierarchy in the Hero section; and make sure CTA buttons have sufficient contrast and hover states that align with the refined logo and color scheme.
acceptanceCriteria: Navbar brand colors and accent tones harmonize with the updated logo colors.
Hero CTA buttons have clearly styled hover/focus states.
Section headings across DairyFeedSection, AgriInputsSection, and CoffeeTradingSection use a consistent typographic hierarchy.
Overall visual impression is cohesive and professional.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/ or frontend/src/components/ui.
Preserve all backend logic in backend/main.mo — no backend changes required.
Keep the existing earthy agricultural OKLCH color palette as the base; only refine or extend it.
nonGoals: Adding new sections or page content.
Changing the site structure or navigation links.
Any backend or data model changes.
Adding animations beyond what already exists.
